hero.title: Construction Project Creation Redesign
hero.subtitle: Optimizing on-site data capture to reduce drop-off rates.

context.title: Context & Challenges
context.project: Project: redesign of the construction creation flow for ChantierPro (Construction SaaS). The goal was to simplify a complex workflow while respecting industry habits.
context.methodology: Methodology: 4-day design challenge, using AI assistance for strategic analysis and workflow efficiency.
objectives: Identify key friction points in the current journey
Deliver a high-fidelity prototype ready for testing

discovery.title: Step 01: Discovery & Strategy
discovery.intro: To prepare the design phase, I combined market research and Synthetic User interviews to align field needs with business stakes.

market.title: Secondary Research (Perplexity)
points: 10% of project budgets lost due to organizational defects.
12 to 16 hours per week spent on admin tasks.
60% to 80% drop-off on overly complex forms.

personas.title: Synthetic Users (simulated interviews)
personas.gaspard: Gaspard — Unstable connection on site, prefers entering data calmly in the evening or at the office.
personas.nadia: Nadia — Uses her phone one-handed with gloves; needs a simple, guided interface.

insights.business: Stopping financial loss: around 10% of a project’s budget is lost due to poor organization.
insights.user: Simplify to retain: 60% to 80% of users drop off when the form becomes too long or complex.

empathy.title: Step 02: Empathy
empathy.intro: Building on field data, I consolidated a primary persona and a user journey map to frame design opportunities on site.

persona.title: Persona — Nicolas B.
persona.caption: Site manager, key field contact for creating and tracking projects.

journey.title: User journey map
journey.caption: End-to-end view of steps, friction points, and critical moments in the current flow.

synthesis.title: Step 03: Synthesis
synthesis.intro: Clustering insights through affinity mapping to turn findings into actionable product levers.
synthesis.affinityCaption: Visual synthesis of recurring themes from research.

points.title: Field ergonomics
points.body: Shift toward voice and tactile input: thick gloves, concrete dust and site noise make classic typing unrealistic.

points.title: Reliability
points.body: Mandatory offline-first mode: connectivity on site is often unstable or unavailable (white zones).

points.title: Adoption barriers
points.body: Move away from office-style project management vocabulary: simplify wording and UI so field teams truly adopt the tool.

userFlow.title: Step 04: Flow Architecture Redesign
userFlow.intro: The main challenge was transforming a rigid linear journey into an adaptive experience suited for field conditions.

old.title: Initial Flow (Audit)
old.legend: A complex journey where every step is mandatory, increasing drop-off risks in low-connectivity areas.

new.title: Optimized Flow (Solution)
new.legend: Agile workflow featuring offline-first mode, voice input, and OCR automation to reduce entry time by 5x.
